title: Platner’s Democratic Support Evaporates After Sexual Assault Allegation
summary: Graham Platner, the Democratic Senate candidate in Maine, called the allegation false but said he would “reflect” on his political path forward, as many allies rescinded their endorsements.

title: In CNN Interview, Woman Details Alleged Sexual Assault by Graham Platner
summary: “This is something that I tried for many years to forget,” the woman, Jenny Racicot, said on CNN.

title: Who Might Replace Graham Platner if He Drops Out of the Maine Senate Race?
summary: Graham Platner can be replaced on the ballot if he withdraws in the next week. If he does, Maine Democrats would face an uncertain two-week race to choose a replacement.

title: Trump Wanted a U.S. Soccer Star to Play in the World Cup. FIFA Found a Way.
summary: There was nothing unusual about a call from President Trump or the reversal of Folarin Balogun’s suspension, said the president of FIFA, Gianni Infantino.

title: In NATO’s Next Act, Can Europe Play the Leading Role?
summary: The focus at a leaders’ summit in Turkey this week will be on building a new model for the alliance, as President Trump pushes to do less and pressures others do more.

title: Canada Bets on NATO Partners for Its Expanded Submarine Fleet
summary: In selecting a joint bid from Germany and Norway, Prime Minister Mark Carney is elevating Canada’s naval power and reducing its military and economic dependency on the United States.

title: McConnell Has Been Hospitalized for 3 Weeks, and Aides Won’t Say Why
summary: Senator Mitch McConnell was admitted to the hospital on June 14, and his office has provided few updates about his condition. Here’s what we know.

title: Michigan Senate Primary Gives Democrats Stark Choice Between Progressive and Moderate Candidate
summary: The state’s important Senate primary just became a head-to-head battle between Abdul El-Sayed, an outspoken left-wing candidate, and Haley Stevens, a moderate backed by party leaders.

title: Inside Trump’s Ideological Fight With the Smithsonian
summary: Publicly and behind the scenes, the president continues to try to impose his own views of American history and culture, presenting an ongoing challenge to Lonnie Bunch, the institution’s leader.

title: Historians Reject Trump Administration’s Criticism of Smithsonian Museum
summary: The nation’s largest group of scholars of U.S. history denounced a White House report attacking the Smithsonian’s National Museum of American History.

title: Joining a Human Tide of Mourners Flowing Through Tehran’s Streets
summary: After days of public mourning ceremonies in the Iranian capital, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s coffin arrived in Tehran’s Azadi Square. This is what I saw covering the miles-long procession on Monday.

title: New Strikes on Ships in Strait of Hormuz Test U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire
summary: A U.S. official said Iranian missiles struck two commercial ships. There was no immediate comment from Iranian officials on the reported attacks.

title: New Videos of Charlie Kirk’s Killing Emerge in Court, but Few Can Watch
summary: On Day 1 of what is expected to be a weeklong hearing, prosecutors played videos of the shooting for a judge, but they were graphic and kept from public view. Erika Kirk and Donald Trump Jr. were in court.

title: Trump Passports Debut, Delighting Some and Dismaying Others
summary: The Washington Passport Agency started issuing the limited-release documents with President Trump’s image on Monday, catching a few applicants by surprise.

title: Trump Tries to Take Credit for Walmart Price Cuts of Beef and Other Products
summary: The retailer said it will lower prices for beef, produce, drinks, pools, toys, grills and summer clothing.

title: Key Health Care Panel Is Again Blocked From Meeting Under RFK Jr.
summary: The United States Preventive Services Task Force has been stymied under Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.

title: The Chinese Company That Could Start a Trade War With Europe
summary: Nuctech’s rise from a Chinese state-backed start-up to a global border-security supplier has made it a lightning rod in trade and subsidy disputes.

title: Samsung Made More Profit Last Quarter Than the Last Two Years Combined
summary: The A.I. boom has propelled the South Korean tech giant’s earnings, but its shares sank as investors had even loftier expectations.

title: Why A.I. Distillation Has Become a Hot Topic in the Race with China
summary: U.S. companies complain that competitors in China are unfairly copying their A.I. systems using a technique that has been around for years.

title: Death Toll From Oppressive Heat in New Jersey May Be as High as 29
summary: In New York City, emergency rooms last week logged the highest number of patients treated for heat-related illnesses in years.

title: Heat Waves Are Taking a Big Toll on Chickens
summary: The birds are particularly vulnerable to high temperatures, and France’s torrid June took a heavy toll.

title: Extreme Heat Hits Europe for the Third Time This Year
summary: It is continent’s third spell of scorching weather since May. Europe is the world’s fastest-warming continent.

title: ‘Confessions II’ Review: Madonna Returns to the Dance Floor
summary: On “Confessions II,” a successor to her hit 2005 album “Confessions on a Dance Floor,” the pop superstar gives new energy to her origin story and shines light on the troubles she’s danced away.

title: Lessons From the Graham Platner Disaster
summary: Many Democrats want representatives as furious as they are. That makes it easier for unfit candidates to escape proper vetting.

title: JD Vance Is Worried
summary: The vice president’s theology comes in book form.

title: ‘Kimmel’ Guest Host Tiffany Haddish Roasts Trump’s Fourth of July Party
summary: The comedian and actress joked that “there was more people at my bat mitzvah” than at America’s 250th birthday party.

title: A Long-Awaited Verdict for Marine Le Pen Could Reshape France’s Politics
summary: A court will decide on Tuesday whether to uphold Ms. Le Pen’s embezzlement conviction. If it does, the far-right leader will probably be barred from running for the presidency next year.
